description: How the product is delivered, operated, and primarily consumed by the customer. Chooses the dominant surface when multiple apply.

values.label: Multi-tenant cloud SaaS
values.definition: Vendor-hosted application shared across customers; access via web and/or mobile apps; vendor runs infra, patching, and scale. Typical PLG or inside-sales motion.

values.label: Dedicated instance / customer VPC
values.definition: Single-tenant deployment logically isolated for one customer—often in the customer’s cloud account (e.g. AWS/Azure/GCP) or a vendor-managed dedicated stack. Strong enterprise procurement and security-review path, weaker PLG.

values.label: Self-hosted / on-prem
values.definition: Customer installs and operates the software in their data center, private cloud, or air-gapped environment; vendor ships binaries, containers, or appliances to run on customer-controlled infra.

values.label: API / SDK / CLI–first platform
values.definition: Primary consumption is programmatic—REST/GraphQL/gRPC, SDKs, CLI, webhooks—often with a thin admin UI. Buyer is typically engineering; land via docs, sandboxes, and integration work.

values.label: Embedded / OEM in customer product
values.definition: Product is built into the customer’s own offering—SDK, UI components, white-label workflows, or data layer—so end-users may not see the vendor brand. Partner and ISV-led GTM.

values.label: Desktop / thick client
values.definition: Installed application on macOS, Windows, or Linux with meaningful local execution (offline, GPU, file system, IDE). Common in design, engineering, security, and media tooling.

values.label: Mobile-primary
values.definition: Core workflows live on iOS/Android; web or API are secondary. Less common in enterprise back-office ICPs but material for field, frontline, or prosumer adjacency.

values.label: Hardware-bound / appliance / device
values.definition: Value requires physical hardware—appliance, sensor, robot, medical device, network gear—with bundled or tightly coupled software. Logistics, certifications, and field ops shape the GTM.

values.label: Managed service with software
values.definition: Ongoing human-operated delivery (monitoring, fulfillment, annotations, implementation pods) paired with a product; customer buys outcomes or retained capacity as much as seats.
